channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate Republicans raise questions after funds for disaster loans exhausted
item.description: A group of GOP senators are pressing the Small Business Administration (SBA) for answers after officials said its disaster loan program exhausted its funds earlier this week in the aftermath of a pair of major hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes: health officials
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hotel attracted would-be judges, ambassadors, pardon-seekers, House Democrats say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Judges seeking appointments to the federal bench. Wealthy Republicans hoping for ambassadorships. Criminals who wanted pardons. They were among the big spenders at Donald Trump ’s Washington, D.C., hotel while he was president.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: BRELAND went to Selma to find himself. His ‘Project 2024' is music based on what he saw and felt
item.description: For BRELAND, the last few years have been a whirlwind. He released his debut album, 2022's “Cross Country,” a fluid approach to country music that now feels prescient — it's the direction the genre continues to move in.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden administration races to shell out billions for clean energy as election nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is shelling out billions of dollars for clean energy and approving major offshore wind projects as officials race to secure major climate initiatives before President Joe Biden's term comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, KREX 5

item.title: Hurricane-proof town in Florida helps residents survive Milton
item.description: "We've really been focused on sustainability, resiliency and innovation," the town's co-founder said.
item.dc:creator: Marni Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: The fine, white sand helps make Florida's beaches among the best in the world. But the powerful storms have turned the precious commodity into a costly nuisance, with sand creating literal barriers to recovery as homeowners and municipalities dig their way out.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California utility shuts off power in 12 counties as 'diablo' wind spikes risk of wildfires
item.description: About 13,000 customers in Northern California woke up without electricity Friday after Pacific Gas and Electric shut off power to prevent its equipment from sparking wildfires amid dry weather and strong winds expected to last through part of the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge rules Delphi composite sketches aren't admissible
item.description: UPDATE: During Friday's proceedings, Judge Fran Gull ruled that the sketches aren't admissible in court. The state filed a motion this week seeking to prohibit the use of the two widely circulated sketches from the early years of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Bret Baier says he made 'a mistake' with Trump clip during Harris interview on Fox News
item.description: Fox News anchor Bret Baier said Thursday that he made "a mistake” in not including a specific clip of former President Trump during his interview with Vice President Harris on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Anti-Jewish rhetoric hits fever pitch in key battleground state
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maine State Police's Lewiston after-action report points out flaws in deadly shooting
item.description: The Maine State Police announced that an independent review of the Lewiston mass shooting after-action report was completed by the New England State Police Administrators Conference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State troopers, NYPD descend on AOC's 'Red Light' neighborhood to clamp down on rampant prostitution, crime
item.description: New York state troopers were deployed to help the NYPD tackle rampant crime and prostitution in a Queens neighborhood, part of which is represented by Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing Texas real estate agent’s husband kept in jail with high bond as family rips his lack of cooperation
item.description: As authorities continue searching a landfill for missing mom Suzanne Simpson, one expert believes husband Brad Simpson is being held on an unusually high bond
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida human trafficking sting nabs 157 people, including 25 illegal immigrants
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said some of the illegal immigrants arrested in the sting allegedly received benefits from the federal government when they entered the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WGN 9

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Memphis cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, Fox 5

item.title: Indian government employee charged in foiled murder-for-hire plot in New York City
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department announced criminal charges Thursday against an Indian government employee who specialized in intelligence in connection with a foiled plot to kill a Sikh separatist leader living in New York City.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parkland shooting judge criticizes shooter's attorneys during talk to law students
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The former judge who presided over the trial of the 2018 Parkland high school mass murderer sharply criticized his public defenders during a presentation to Miami law students Thursday, saying they acted unprofessionally and crossed several lines while representing him.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NOAA releases winter weather outlook: What Southern California can expect
item.description: Depending on where you live in the U.S., this winter could bring a mix of snow and rain or an unseasonably warm start to the season, according to climate experts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado gold mine where tour guide was killed and tourists trapped ordered closed by regulators
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado gold mine where a tour guide was killed and a group of tourists was trapped for hours after an elevator accident has been ordered to remain closed and not conduct tours while its operations are reviewed, state regulators said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google Flights adds 'Cheapest' option for travelers who just don't care how they get there
item.description: Google Flights has a new option for immediately finding the “cheapest” flight itinerary, but it may come at a slightly different cost.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma parents and teachers sue to stop top education official's classroom Bible mandate
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A group of Oklahoma parents of public school students, teachers and ministers filed a lawsuit Thursday seeking to stop the state's top education official from forcing schools to incorporate the Bible into lesson plans for students in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years, but the parents of a Missouri second grader say she's already had her first crash. Seven-year-old Alannah Cheffen, of Independence, said she crashed her school bus into another bus on school grounds.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KTLA 5

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials in Argentina have released a cause of death for Liam Payne, the former One Direction member who died on Wednesday at the age of 31.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Texas man overcomes testicular cancer twice, pursues dream of fatherhood
item.description: "Testicular cancer can grow very quickly. People notice this difference over about one to two weeks," Dr. Kyle Keyes said.
item.dc:creator: Jose Torres, NewsNation
